text.title: Go Layout Examples and Antipatterns
text.summary: Worked example of a handler with injected service interface, plus antipatterns to avoid.

example.note: Handler depends on an interface it defines. The real service.UserService satisfies it implicitly.

antipattern.description: All code in a single package at the repo root (flat structure).
antipattern.reason: No package boundaries means any file can call any function. Adding internal/ later requires touching every import.

antipattern.description: A single "utils" or "common" package that grows into a God package imported by everyone.
antipattern.reason: God packages create circular dependency risks and make the codebase harder to understand and test in isolation.

antipattern.description: Importing a parent layer from a child (e.g. repository importing service).
antipattern.reason: Reverse dependencies create circular import errors or force awkward interface gymnastics to break the cycle.
